Issues: File appears truncated/incomplete: the last entry ([11]) cuts off mid-field, which will break BibTeX parsing unless fixed.
DBLP-style nonstandard/extra fields (timestamp, biburl, bibsource) are included; usually harmless but can be undesirable/noisy depending on bibliography style.
Key naming is consistent but DBLP-centric (DBLP:...); valid for BibTeX, though not the most conventional authorYear style some users prefer.
Some venues use abbreviated journal names (e.g., "IEEE Trans Autom. Sci. Eng."); acceptable but may be inconsistent with styles requiring full titles.
LLM evaluation.Comment: The shown entries are well-formed BibTeX (bracing, commas, escaping like {\"{o}}, appropriate entry types, and strong metadata including DOI/URL/pages/volume/number). Main concern is usability due to the apparent truncation/incomplete final entry, which would prevent direct LaTeX/BibTeX compilation until repaired.

eval_report.comment: Excellent! BibTeX file fully covers all papers with proper format and accurate field information.

import re
with open('/tmp/isolated_workspace_1091/ref.bib') as f:

entries = re.findall(r'@\w+\{[^@]+', content)

{"command": "python3 -c "
import re
with open('/tmp/isolated_workspace_1091/ref.bib') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Extract entries with paper number and first few lines
sections = content.split('% [')
for s in sections[1:]:
    num = s.split(']')[0]
    # Get entry type and key
    m = re.search(r'@(\w+)\{(\S+),', s)
    if m:
        etype = m.group(1)
        ekey = m.group(2)
        # Get title
        tm = re.search(r'title\s*=\s*\{(.+?)[\},]', s, re.DOTALL)
